summary: Abies grandis ( grand fir, giant fir, lowland white fir, great silver fir, western white fir, Vancouver fir, or Oregon fir ) is a fir native to northwestern North America, occurring at altitudes of sea level to 1,700 metres (5,600 ft). It is a major constituent of the Grand Fir/Douglas Fir Ecoregion of the Cascade Range.
